# Generated Files

This guide explains what `claude-remote-config` writes in a consuming project.

## Human-authored files

This file is created by `setup` when missing and should be committed:

```text
.claude-remote-config.json
```

`.claude-remote-config.json` selects the remote, optional ref, output flags, and tags.

## Generated and gitignored paths

These paths are generated by `init` and are normally gitignored:

```text
.claude/rules/remote/
.claude/commands/remote/
.claude/skills/remote-*/
.claude/settings.local.json
.claude/hooks.local.json
.claude-remote-config/cache/
```

`.claude/rules/remote/` contains generated Claude Code rule files mirroring the remote `.claude/rules/` subtree.

`.claude/commands/remote/` contains generated Claude Code command files. Note: Claude Code resolves command names by filename stem only, so `/remote/testing/test.md` exposes `/test`, not `/remote/testing/test`.

`.claude/skills/remote-*/` contains generated Claude Code skill directories with a `remote-` prefix.

`.claude/settings.local.json` contains generated local Claude Code settings, replaced entirely on each sync.

`.claude/hooks.local.json` contains generated local Claude Code hooks, replaced entirely on each sync.

`.claude-remote-config/cache/` stores cached remote git repositories when the plugin data directory is inside the project.

## `.gitignore`

`setup` and `init` add a `claude-remote-config` block to `.gitignore` unless `--no-gitignore` is used.

The block is idempotent. Running the command again does not duplicate it.
